final_answer:
Yes, there are still many BSC/BNB references in core code.

I scanned core + genesis-contract and found:
- `~73` files in `bsc/` with `bsc/bnb` references
- `~28` files in `bsc-genesis-contract/` with `bsc/bnb` references

Main places to scrub first:
- Consensus/network identity in [config.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/params/config.go#L660), [config.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/params/config.go#L783), [config.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/params/config.go#L803)
- Consensus engine comments/logic hooks in [parlia.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/consensus/parlia/parlia.go#L242), [parlia.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/consensus/parlia/parlia.go#L1493), [feynmanfork.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/consensus/parlia/feynmanfork.go#L99)
- System-contract upgrade metadata hardcoded to `bsc-genesis-contract` commits in [upgrade.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/core/systemcontracts/upgrade.go#L111)
- Genesis project naming and contract class names in [package.json](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/package.json#L2), [pyproject.toml](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/pyproject.toml#L2), [BSCValidatorSet.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/BSCValidatorSet.sol#L16), [BSCGovernor.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/BSCGovernor.sol#L17), [BSCTimelock.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/BSCTimelock.sol#L9), [check-bsc-hardfork-bytecode.ts](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/scripts/check-bsc-hardfork-bytecode.ts)

Consensus type:
- It is **Parlia PoSA** (proof-of-staked-authority), with **DPoS-style election** behavior.
- Source: [config.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/params/config.go#L783), [feynmanfork.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/consensus/parlia/feynmanfork.go#L99)

How new validators are added:
- Validator self-registers via `createValidator` with self-stake + lock in [StakeHub.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/StakeHub.sol#L493)
- Minimum self stake currently set to `2,000` native units in [StakeHub.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/StakeHub.sol#L413)
- Validator election info is read from StakeHub and top voting-power validators are selected in [StakeHub.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/StakeHub.sol#L1575), [feynmanfork.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/consensus/parlia/feynmanfork.go#L104)
- Validator set is updated by consensus daily on breathe-block boundary in [parlia.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc/consensus/parlia/parlia.go#L1493)

How delegation works now:
- Native token delegation (your GILT runtime coin) via `delegate/undelegate/claim` in [StakeHub.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/StakeHub.sol#L671)
- GOLD-side delegation via ERC1155 path `delegateTokenB1155/undelegateTokenB1155` in [StakeHub.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/StakeHub.sol#L726)
- Effective voting power combines native stake + GOLD stake via [StakeHub.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bsc-genesis-contract/contracts/StakeHub.sol#L2323)

If you want, next step I can give you a surgical rename plan split into:
1. Safe rename without chain reset
2. Full clean rename that requires stop/rebuild/restart.

Read up on the code of the core blockchain, then tell me where all this is mentioned
